ops.title: Three verified gotchas for browser screenshots and local daemons on this machine (agent-browser screenshot hangs; SSE blocks networkidle; unix sockets need short $HOME)
ops.body:
Verified across the cc-present UX overhaul (2026-07):

1. **agent-browser's `screenshot` subcommand hangs indefinitely** on this machine (headless Chrome for Testing 147 / swiftshader), even on `about:blank`; its navigation/click/eval commands work fine. Capture screenshots by driving the same bundled binary with playwright-core: `executablePath '/Users/yasyf/.agent-browser/browsers/chrome-147.0.7727.57/Google Chrome for Testing.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome for Testing'` (working scripts in /tmp/pwshot/).
2. **Never wait on `networkidle` for SPAs holding an SSE stream** (cc-present, cc-review): the `/events` connection never idles. Wait for a concrete selector plus a fixed settle.
3. **Daemons binding unix sockets need a short `$HOME`** (macOS `sun_path` limit is ~104 bytes). Session scratchpad paths are far too long — run isolated daemons under `/tmp/<short>` and tear them down after.

**Why:** each cost a debugging round before an agent found the workaround; they will bite any browser-verification or daemon-isolation task on this machine.

**How to apply:** put all three in the prompt of any agent doing browser verification against a local daemon; prefer the playwright-core capture path from the start. Related: [[background-agents-hang-on-permission-prompts]].
